title: grevillea bracteosa subsp. howatharra
intro: Grevillea bracteosa subsp. howatharra, also known as Howatharra grevillea, is a subspecies of shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its dense, compact growth habit and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, with its leaves and flowers believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves of the Howatharra grevillea have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Grevillea bracteosa subsp. howatharra can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a tea blend, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and medicinal uses.
